title: Contacts
lede: Contacts are the real people in your book of business — every one of them one click away from a call, an email, or a text. This guide walks you A to Z through adding, organizing, filtering, and cleaning up contacts in the iMash CRM.

steps.title: Open the Contacts list
steps.content:
Everything starts at the Contacts list — a live table of every person you do business with, with calling built right in.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then click the Contacts tab (or go directly to /crm/contacts). The tab badge shows your total contact count.
  2. Each row shows the contact's Avatar, Name (with their primary email underneath), Email, Phone, Stage, Account (their company), and Last Updated.
  3. In the Phone column, click any blue number to call the contact instantly through the web dialer. If they have several numbers, click +N more to pick one.
  4. The Actions column gives you three one-click shortcuts per row: the green eye opens the profile, the blue phone starts a call, and the red trash deletes the contact (with a confirmation).
  5. Click any column header (Name, Email, Stage, Account, Last Updated) to sort by it; click again to flip the direction.
💡 A Contact is a person. The company they work for lives in Accounts, and prospects who haven't converted yet live in Leads.

images.alt: The Contacts list showing avatars, names, phones, stages, and accounts
images.caption: The Contacts list — search, filters, bulk tools, and click-to-call in one screen

images.alt: The Contacts list showing avatars, names, phones, stages, and accounts
images.caption: The Contacts list — search, filters, bulk tools, and click-to-call in one screen

steps.title: Add a new contact
steps.content:
1. Click + Add Contact in the toolbar at the top-right of the list. The Create Contact drawer slides in from the right.
  2. Enter the First Name and Last Name — these are the only two required fields.
  3. Optionally pick an Account to link the person to their company (type to search; each option shows the company logo).
  4. Choose a Stage — it defaults to New and tracks where this person is in your relationship.
  5. Fill in the mailing address if you have it: Street Address, Apartment/Suite, City, State/Province, Postal Code, and Country.
  6. Click Save in the bottom-right. The drawer closes and your new contact appears in the list.
💡 If you pick United States as the country, the State/Province field turns into a dropdown of US states — otherwise it stays free text.

images.alt: The Create Contact drawer with name, emails, phone numbers, account, stage, and address fields
images.caption: The Create Contact drawer — only First Name and Last Name are required

images.alt: The Create Contact drawer with name, emails, phone numbers, account, stage, and address fields
images.caption: The Create Contact drawer — only First Name and Last Name are required

steps.title: Add multiple emails and phone numbers
steps.content:
Real people have more than one way to reach them — iMash stores as many emails and phone numbers per contact as you need.

  1. In the Emails field, click + Add Email for each address. The first email in the list is the primary one — it's what appears in the list view and is used by default when you email the contact. Reorder to change which is primary.
  2. In the Phone Numbers field, click Add Phone Number. A small dialog opens.
  3. Type the Phone Number and pick a Label — Mobile, Home, Work, Office, Other, Landline, Personal, or Fax.
  4. Click Save. The number appears as a labeled entry; use the red trash icon next to it to remove it.
  5. Repeat for every number the contact has — all of them become click-to-call in the list and profile.

images.alt: The Add Phone Number dialog with a phone number field and a label dropdown
images.caption: Add as many numbers as you need, each with its own label

images.alt: The Add Phone Number dialog with a phone number field and a label dropdown
images.caption: Add as many numbers as you need, each with its own label

steps.title: Search, filter, and sort the list
steps.content:
Find anyone in seconds, even in a list of thousands.

  1. Use the dropdown to the left of the search box to choose what to search by: Name, Email, or Phone. Then type in Search Contacts — results update as you type.
  2. To narrow further, open the Filter By dropdown and tick one or more filter types. Each one adds its own control next to the toolbar:
  • Stage — show only contacts in a specific lifecycle stage.
  • Account — show contacts linked to one or more companies (type to search accounts).
  • Tags — filter by a tag; this also adds a Tags column to the table.
  • Address — free-text match across street, city, state, postal code, and country; adds an Address column.
  • City — match on city only; adds a City column.
  • Owner — show contacts owned by specific team members.
💡 Phone search is forgiving — it matches partial numbers and ignores dashes and spaces, so 555010 finds +1 (555) 010-7788.

images.alt: The Contacts toolbar with search field selector, search box, and Filter By dropdown
images.caption: The toolbar: search-by selector, search box, and Filter By

images.alt: The Contacts toolbar with search field selector, search box, and Filter By dropdown
images.caption: The toolbar: search-by selector, search box, and Filter By

images.alt: The toolbar with Stage and Tags filters enabled, showing their extra dropdown controls
images.caption: Enabling Stage and Tags in Filter By adds their own dropdowns to the toolbar

images.alt: The toolbar with Stage and Tags filters enabled, showing their extra dropdown controls
images.caption: Enabling Stage and Tags in Filter By adds their own dropdowns to the toolbar

steps.title: Open a contact's profile
steps.content:
Click any contact's name or avatar to open the Contact Details drawer — the full 360° view of that person.

  1. The header shows the avatar (click the camera icon to upload a photo), the contact's name, current stage pill, primary email, and primary phone.
  2. Use the round buttons in the top-right of the header: the blue phone button calls the contact, the envelope button starts an email.
  3. The Contact Information tab is where you edit the essentials inline — no separate edit mode needed:
  • Emails — the same multi-email editor; the entry marked Primary (starred, first in the list) is the default address.
  • Account — click to search and link the contact to a company.
  • Tags — type a tag and press Enter to add it (for example VIP or renewal); click the × on a tag to remove it. Tags power the Tags filter in the list.
  • Phone Numbers — pick a label, type the number, and click Add; the trash icon deletes a number.
  • Timezone — set the contact's local timezone so calls and calendar events display at the right local time.

images.alt: The Contact Details drawer showing the header and the Contact Information tab with emails, account, tags, phone numbers, and timezone
images.caption: The Contact Information tab — everything is editable inline

images.alt: The Contact Details drawer showing the header and the Contact Information tab with emails, account, tags, phone numbers, and timezone
images.caption: The Contact Information tab — everything is editable inline

steps.title: Explore the profile tabs
steps.content:
The tab row across the profile turns each contact into a complete customer file:

  • Additional Information — edit the name, full mailing address (street, apartment/suite, city, state, postal code, country), and tax settings (Tax-Exempt plus Exemption Certificate #).
  • Call Logs — every call with this contact; click a row to open the full call detail.
  • Files — attach documents, photos, and contracts to the contact.
  • Custom Data — free-form custom variables your AI agents and automations can read and write.
  • Calendar — the contact's calendar events; click Add Event to schedule a new one with them.
  • Email — the full email conversation with this contact; badges on the tab show total and unread counts.
  • Deals, Invoices, Quotes, Jobs — every pipeline record linked to this person.
  • Payment Methods — cards saved on file for this contact, ready for one-click charging.
💡 Changes in the info tabs save as you make them — there is no separate Save button on the profile.

images.alt: The Additional Information tab showing name and address fields
images.caption: The Additional Information tab — name, address, and tax details

images.alt: The Additional Information tab showing name and address fields
images.caption: The Additional Information tab — name, address, and tax details

steps.title: Track the lifecycle stage and add notes
steps.content:
Scroll to the bottom of the profile to manage where this person stands and what your team should know.

  1. Under Lifecycle, the stage bar shows the journey from New through Contacted, Interested, Qualified, Negotiation, and Won. The current stage is highlighted in color.
  2. Click a stage to move the contact there. Stages with a small arrow open a dropdown of sub-stages (for example Contacted → No Answer, Voicemail, Wrong Number) — pick the exact one.
  3. If the contact is enrolled in campaigns with their own stage sets, read-only Campaign Lifecycles bars appear below, one per campaign.
  4. Under Notes, type in Add your note and press Enter. Notes are timestamped, so anyone opening the profile gets the story at a glance.
💡 Your stage names and colors are configurable in CRM Settings, so your bar may show custom stages instead of the defaults.

images.alt: The lifecycle stage bar showing New highlighted and the following stages with sub-stage dropdown arrows
images.caption: Click a stage to update it — arrows open sub-stage choices

images.alt: The lifecycle stage bar showing New highlighted and the following stages with sub-stage dropdown arrows
images.caption: Click a stage to update it — arrows open sub-stage choices

steps.title: Work in bulk: owner, account, delete
steps.content:
1. Tick the checkbox on individual rows, or tick Select all N contacts in the toolbar to select every contact matching your current search and filters — across all pages. Untick specific rows to exclude them.
  2. As soon as something is selected, three bulk buttons appear next to the Selected N counter:
  • Change Owner (person icon) — reassign all selected contacts to another team member.
  • Assign Account (card icon) — link every selected contact to one company; leave the account empty and click Assign to clear their account instead.
  • Delete Selected (red trash icon) — remove all selected contacts after a confirmation.
⚠️ Bulk delete is permanent and can cover thousands of records when Select all is on — double-check the count in the confirmation dialog before you confirm.

images.alt: The toolbar with Select all checked, showing Selected count and the Change Owner, Assign Account, and Delete buttons
images.caption: With contacts selected, the bulk buttons appear: owner, account, delete

images.alt: The toolbar with Select all checked, showing Selected count and the Change Owner, Assign Account, and Delete buttons
images.caption: With contacts selected, the bulk buttons appear: owner, account, delete

images.alt: The Assign Account to Selected Contacts dialog with an account search dropdown
images.caption: Assign Account links every selected contact to one company in a single click

images.alt: The Assign Account to Selected Contacts dialog with an account search dropdown
images.caption: Assign Account links every selected contact to one company in a single click

steps.title: Find and remove duplicates
steps.content:
Duplicate records waste dials and split your history. The built-in duplicate finder keeps your list spotless.

  1. Click the copy icon (Find Duplicates) in the toolbar. The Find & Remove Duplicate Contacts dialog opens.
  2. Pick a Duplicate scope: Across all contacts (any matching phone/email) or Within same account only.
  3. Click Phone Number or Email Address to run the scan.
  4. Matches appear in groups with summary tiles for Duplicate Groups and Total Duplicates to Remove. In each group the oldest contact is marked Keep and the rest Delete.
  5. Click Delete N Duplicate(s) on a single group, or Delete All Duplicates to clean everything at once — both ask for confirmation first.
⚠️ Deleting duplicates cannot be undone. The oldest record in each group is always the one kept.

images.alt: The Find and Remove Duplicate Contacts dialog with scope selector and Phone Number / Email Address buttons
images.caption: Scan by phone or email, across all contacts or within the same account

images.alt: The Find and Remove Duplicate Contacts dialog with scope selector and Phone Number / Email Address buttons
images.caption: Scan by phone or email, across all contacts or within the same account

steps.title: Import contacts and connect via API
steps.content:
Bringing an existing list into iMash takes a minute.

  1. Click the upload icon (Import) in the toolbar to open Import Contacts from CSV.
  2. Click or drag your CSV file into the upload area. The file needs headers in the first row and must include First Name, Last Name, and either Email or Phone.
  3. Click Next to map your CSV columns to contact fields, then finish the import — your contacts appear in the list immediately.
  4. Prefer code? Click the link icon (API Info) in the toolbar for ready-to-copy API examples to create and query contacts programmatically.

That's it — your entire book of business is now in one place, one click from every call, message, and invoice. 🚀

images.alt: The Import Contacts from CSV dialog with a drag-and-drop upload area
images.caption: Drag in a CSV with First Name, Last Name, and Email or Phone

images.alt: The Import Contacts from CSV dialog with a drag-and-drop upload area
images.caption: Drag in a CSV with First Name, Last Name, and Email or Phone

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
